fix: handle version race in crawler as skip, not failure (#281)

When the nightly crawl and tracker system process the same repo
concurrently, the tracker may publish a version first. The crawler
then hits a VersionConflictError which was caught by a broad
except Exception, logged as a noisy warning with full traceback,
and counted as "failed".

Catch VersionConflictError specifically and return "skipped" with
an INFO-level log, matching the tracker path's behavior (PR #272).

Fixes #280

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix filter chip tooltips not translating to Turkish

Tooltips used hardcoded data-tip-desc attributes. Added data-tip-key
attributes that the tooltip handler resolves via t() for localization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: increase check_trackers timeout from 600s to 900s (#279)

When tracker_process_repo timeout was raised from 300s to 600s, the
parent check_trackers function kept its 600s timeout. A single slow
child container (up to 660s with cold start) can now exceed the parent
timeout, killing it mid-flight and cascading cancellation to other
in-progress containers.

Increase check_trackers to 900s and update the budget comment.
New math: (180-30) + ~30 + 660 = 840 < 900 ✓

Fixes #278

* fix(finops-plugin,git-plugin): fix pipe and redirection errors in context commands

Replace `git remote -v | head -1` and `git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | sed ...`
patterns with `git remote get-url origin` to eliminate pipe and redirection operator
violations in skill context commands. Also remove complex owner-type detection command
that used multiple prohibited operators (pipes, redirections, ||).

Context commands run in a restricted execution engine that blocks pipe operators,
redirection operators, and shell operators.

commit.message:
feat: Vedana — agent's sukham/duhkham health pulse (BG 13.6-7)

Sukham (pleasure) and Duhkham (pain) are listed in Gita 13.6-7 as
part of the Ksetra (material field). They CAN and MUST be programmed.

Vedana is the single health pulse of the agent itself (not the codebase —
that's HealthSense/GHRANA). It reads from all internal sources:
  - Provider health (alive/total, 35% weight — most critical)
  - Error pressure (recent error rate, 25% weight)
  - Context pressure (context window usage, 15% weight)
  - Synaptic confidence (Hebbian weight average, 15% weight)
  - Tool success rate (execution outcomes, 10% weight)

Produces VedanaSignal with 0.0-1.0 health + guna derivation:
  > 0.7 = sukham/sattva, 0.3-0.7 = rajas, < 0.3 = duhkham/tamas

Wired into StewardAgent:
  - agent.vedana property reads live health
  - get_state() includes vedana breakdown

Prerequisite for Cetana (heartbeat adapts to health) and BuddyBubble
(peers read each other's vedana).

13 behavioral tests (zero dataclass inspection).

commit.message:
release: deploy sport-specific tick data routing to production (#197)

* feat: add Terraform-managed Claude Code Spot VM for 24/7 async development

Provision a GCP Spot VM that runs Claude Code CLI as a systemd daemon,
polling GitHub issues labelled 'claude' and autonomously implementing
changes on feature branches with strict SDLC compliance.

Infrastructure (all gated behind var.claude_dev_enabled):
- Spot VM (e2-standard-4) with SSD, auto-STOP on preemption
- Secret Manager secrets for Anthropic API key and GitHub PAT
- Cloud Function + Cloud Scheduler for auto-restart every 30 min
- IAM binding for Secret Manager access

SDLC enforcement:
- Agent only pushes to feature/* branches, never develop or main
- Tests must pass before PR creation; failures abort with a comment
- GitHub branch protection rules block direct pushes (documented)
- Conventional Commits enforced via prompt injection

Files:
- terraform/claude_dev_vm.tf — all Terraform resources
- terraform/scripts/claude-vm-startup.sh — VM bootstrap script
- terraform/functions/claude_dev_restarter/ — restart Cloud Function
- scripts/claude-agent-loop.js — agent daemon (polls issues, runs Claude)
- docs/CLAUDE_CODE_SPOT_VM.md — full operational plan

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Claude Code Spot VM for 24/7 autonomous development (#180)

* feat: use Claude Pro subscription auth instead of API key for spot VM

Remove anthropic-api-key secret from Terraform and startup script.
Claude Code now authenticates via Pro subscription OAuth session

late-catalogue migration, and the no-metadata fallback.

commit.message:
ci: auto-publish Docker image to GHCR (#283)

* ci: add Docker image auto-publish to GHCR

Adds GitHub Actions workflow to build and push Docker images to
GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io/zylos-ai/zylos-core).

Triggers:
- Push tag v* → publishes :v0.3.6, :0.3, :latest
- Push to main (Docker-related files only) → publishes :latest

Features:
- Multi-platform: linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 (covers NAS/ARM users)
- Build cache via GitHub Actions cache (faster rebuilds)
- Uses GITHUB_TOKEN (no extra secrets needed)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: use :main tag for dev builds, :latest only on release tags

- Push to main → :main (testing/dev)
- Push tag v* → :1.2.3, :1.2, :latest (stable release)

Follows the pattern used by Immich, Open WebUI, and Dify.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: install zylos-core from local source instead of GitHub URL

The Dockerfile was doing `npm install -g https://github.com/zylos-ai/zylos-core`
which always installs main HEAD, regardless of which tag/commit is being
built. Changed to COPY the local source and install from it, so the image
always matches the exact commit/tag being built.

This aligns with how install.sh resolves the latest release tag, and is
the standard pattern for Dockerfile builds in CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: remove build directive from docker-compose.yml

The compose file is meant for end users who curl-download it and run
`docker compose up -d`. It should only reference the pre-built GHCR
image, not include build configuration.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

produced NaN for all coordinates.

hydrates. Switch to client:load so it hydrates on page load, fetches
/api/clients, and renders.
